preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced bilabial plosive [b] to a close front rounded vowel [y] represents an extreme case of lenition and vocalization, involving a significant shift in both manner and place of articulation.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves lenition, where the plosive consonant [b] becomes a vowel [y]

examples.note: In Shehret, the vowel /y/ is documented as a reflex of historical *b in a closed set of lexemes.

sources: Source via Google Search grounding (verify before merge)

related.label: Vocalization to close back rounded vowel
